title: Close out Phase 1: final review, dry-run the new retro on this epic, flesh out Phase 2 (hbz) into ticks
description:
The close-out task per the convention this very epic introduces (spec §1, docs/design/execution-model-v2-epic-chaining-and-learning-loop.md).

1. Final review of the epic's full diff (skills/ticks/ changes): consistency across SKILL.md, claude-runner.md, tick-patterns.md — same terminology, no duplicated or contradicting guidance, cross-references resolve.
2. Dry-run the freshly written retro procedure on this epic itself (first real use): harvest tick notes, create .tick/learnings.md if anything qualifies, do outside-in verification of spec §1/§2/§4/§5/§6 against the actual skill text.
3. Sync installed copy: the live skill at ~/.claude/skills/ticks/ must match skills/ticks/ (check how installation/sync works in this repo — scripts/ or install docs — and follow it).
4. Flesh out Phase 2 (epic hbz) into ticks per its rough scope, using the new partitioning procedure from tick-patterns.md.

Note: hbz is NOT gated (--awaiting), so per the new two-tier stopping rule, execution flows on into Phase 2.
acceptance_criteria: Epic diff reviewed for cross-file consistency; retro dry-run done (learnings file created if warranted); hbz has child ticks created with the new procedure
closed_reason: Close-out executed: epic diff reviewed (1 inconsistency found+fixed), retro dry-run done (.tick/learnings.md created with 2 orchestration entries), installed skill synced to ~/.claude/skills/ticks, Phase 2 (hbz) fleshed into 5 ticks (3 waves) with foundation-first procedure
